label: Shoulder bursitis without septic-joint or trauma red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated shoulder bursitis.
found: Your clinician diagnosed shoulder bursitis. We did not find septic joint concern, fracture or dislocation concern, infection, nerve or blood-flow problems, or another shoulder emergency today.
home: Rest the shoulder from activity that makes pain worse.
Use ice, heat, gentle range-of-motion, support, or activity limits only as your clinician recommended.
Return to normal activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use medicine to push through worsening pain, fever, weakness, or swelling.
return: Fever, a red hot joint, rapidly worsening swelling, severe pain, or feeling very ill.
New arm numbness, weakness, blue or pale hand, deformity, or inability to use the shoulder.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated shoulder bursitis.
No septic joint concern, skin infection, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Septic joint or infection concern, cellulitis, open wound, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, severe or rapidly worsening pain, high-energy trauma, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports bursitis framing, rest/activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and escalation for infection, severe pain, or functional worsening. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated shoulder bursitis with septic joint, infection, trauma, fracture/dislocation, neurovascular, pregnancy/pediatric, and specialist-directed pathways absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated shoulder bursitis
no septic joint or infection concern
no fracture/dislocation concern or high-energy trauma
intact neurovascular exam
